title: 100 Francs - Cézanne (type 1997)

obverse.description: Portrait of Paul Cézanne (1838 –1906); in the background the evocation of "La mer à l'Estaque", a painting painted in 1878-1879; above the watermark a view of Jas de Bouffan, a family property; below a representation of the canvas "The Card Players". To the right of the Jas de Bouffan is a foil strip segmented into four and a solid security thread.
obverse.lettering:
100 Cent Francs
Paul Cézanne
1839-1906
100
Banque de France
obverse.lettering_translation: One Hundred Francs, Paul Cézanne, Bank of France

reverse.description: An interpretation of "Apples and Cookies" canvas painted in 1880; above the watermark a chromatic circle and below "The card players" extracted from the famous painting
reverse.lettering:
Banque de France
100
100 Cent Francs
LA CONTREFAÇON OU LA FALSIFICATION DES BILLETS DE BANQUE ET LA MISE EN CIRCULATION DES BILLETS CONTREFAITS OU FALSIFIÉS SONT PUNIS PAR LES ARTICLES 442-1 et 442-2 DU CODE PÉNAL DE PEINES ALLANT JUSQU'À TRENTE ANS DE RÉCLUSION CRIMINELLE ET TROIS MILLIONS DE FRANCS D'AMENDE.
reverse.lettering_translation:
Bank of France
One Hundred Francs
Counterfeiting or falsification of banknotes and the release of counterfeit and falsified notes are punishable by articles 442-1 and 442-2 or the penal code of sentences of up to 30 years of criminal reclusion and a 3 million franc fine.

comments:
One combination of signatories :
a) Didier Bruneel – Jean Bonnardin – Yves Barroux (1997 et 1998)

The jug near the top-left corner of the obverse side is printed in colour-shifting ink - changes colours from jade green to turquoise:
